_note: A foreign corpus for `cairn import`, transcribed from the public baseline carlomartinucci/flf-epistack-contest @ 774c9ee -- specifically the shared-upstream map the 28-line probe extracted (internal prior-art notes/baseline-probe/OUTPUT.txt in the writeup repo). This is the anchor's OWN natural-origin evidence items and the DOIs each cites; slugs and edges are the baseline's, not ours. It is the subset of its 15 natural-origin items that appears in at least one shared upstream. `verify_baseline_import.py` re-derives the per-DOI counts from this file and checks they reproduce the probe (5/5/3/3/3/2/2/2), so the transcription is a checked artifact, not a remembered one.

claims.text: Early COVID-19 cases show no clustering away from the Huanan market (natural-origin evidence item).
SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples co-locate with wildlife stalls at the market (natural-origin evidence item).
The earliest known cases cluster spatially around the Huanan market (natural-origin evidence item).
Both viral lineages (A and B) center spatially on the Huanan market (natural-origin evidence item).
Lineages A and B reflect at least two separate zoonotic introductions (natural-origin evidence item).
The molecular-clock TMRCA estimate places the common ancestor around November 2019 (natural-origin evidence item).
The two-lineage structure implies multiple introductions rather than one (natural-origin evidence item).
Genetic tracing places susceptible species (e.g. raccoon dogs) at positive market stalls (natural-origin evidence item).
SARS-CoV-1 emerged via a live-animal market, a precedent for market origin (natural-origin evidence item).
SARS-CoV-1 and MERS both had zoonotic origins, a precedent (natural-origin evidence item).
